entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: A country in East Africa where child malnutrition is widespread and the study was conducted

entities.name: CHILD MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A widespread health issue in Ethiopia affecting children

entities.name: NUTRITION KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: Knowledge possessed by women regarding nutrition, identified as a key impact pathway for child nutrition

entities.name: EMPOWERMENT
entities.description: The process of increasing women's autonomy and decision-making power, identified as a key impact pathway for child nutrition

entities.name: CHILD NUTRITION OUTCOMES
entities.description: The measurable results of nutritional status in children

entities.name: ECONOMETRIC STRATEGIES
entities.description: Statistical methods used to analyze survey data while accounting for endogeneity concerns

entities.name: DIETARY DIVERSITY
entities.description: A measure of the variety of foods consumed by children

entities.name: STUNTING
entities.description: A condition of impaired growth and development in children due to chronic malnutrition

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD AGRICULTURAL DECISIONS
entities.description: The role of women in making decisions related to farming and agriculture within the home

entities.name: ECONOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Access to and control over financial assets by women
